content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>幫我查 sync.ps1 確認 steering 改 junction 會不會有衝突</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-22</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
確認了關鍵事實:

- `~/.kiro/steering/skill-workflow.md`、`~/.claude/steering/skill-workflow.md`、`G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\skill-workflow.md` — 三份**長度都是 1671、都沒有 LinkType**。
- 也就是說它們是**三份獨立複製檔**,不是 junction/symlink。

這點很關鍵,因為你的 skills 是 **junction**(改正本即時生效),但 steering 是 **copy**(要手動 `sync.ps1` 才會更新)。兩者機制不同 — 這正是「直接寫在 `~/.kiro/steering/`」的缺點來源。

## 直接寫在 `~/.kiro/steering/skill-workflow.md` 的缺點

**1. 會被下次 sync 覆蓋(最致命)**
steering 是 copy。你跑 `G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1 -Apply` 時,它用正本內容覆寫 `~/.kiro/steering/`。你直接在這裡的編輯會**無聲消失**。這其實就是你自己 doc 裡白紙黑字的禁止事項:

> ❌ 直接在 `~/.kiro/skills/` 或 `~/.claude/skills/` 裡新增或編輯（會被下次 sync 覆蓋）

同樣邏輯完全適用 steering copy。

**2. 三份會發散,失去 single source of truth**
canonical / .kiro / .claude 是三份獨立檔。改任一份,另外兩份不同步。你的整套設計前提是「正本是唯一 source of truth,投影可重建」— 改投影就破壞了這個前提。

**3. 不進版控**
正本在 `AI-canonical` git repo(你會 commit + push,有歷史)。projection 的編輯不被追蹤、不會 push,換機或重跑 sync 就丟。

**4. 與 skills 心智模型不一致,容易誤判**
skills 改正本即時反映(junction),steering 改正本要手動 sync(copy)。你可能誤以為「改了就生效」,但 steering 其實沒 sync 就還是舊的。

## 正確作法
改 `G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\skill-workflow.md` → `sync.ps1 -Apply` → `cd G:\AI\AI-canonical && git commit && git push`。

## 但有個更上游的問題值得一起想

就算你完全照正確流程做(改正本 + sync + push),**Telegram bridge 還是讀不到 steering**(上一輪確認過,bridge 只備份不讀取)。所以對「透過 Telegram 進來的 agent」而言,steering 改了等於沒改。

順帶一提:既然 steering 跟 skills 都是「正本→投影」模型,**為什麼 skills 用 junction 而 steering 用 copy?** 如果 steering 也改成 junction,就能消掉上面缺點 1~4(改正本即時生效、無覆蓋風險、無發散)。這可能是當初設計的一個不一致點,值得你決定要不要統一。

要我幫你查 `sync.ps1` 確認 steering 改 junction 會不會有衝突(例如 `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` 的 `@steering/...` import 路徑),還是先處理 bridge 讀 steering 那個斷點?

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: 檢查 sync.ps1 steering 改 junction 衝突

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T09:39:18.050Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical\\\\tools\\\\sync.ps1\",\"content\":\"#requires -Version 5\\n<#\\n  sync.ps1 — 從 canonical 正本投影 skills 到各 CLI(Windows junction)\\n\\n  來源(兩正本的 skills/<domain>/<name>):\\n    個人 general : $PersonalRoot\\\\skills\\\\general\\\\*\\n    公司 slot    : $CorpRoot\\\\skills\\\\slot\\\\*\\n  目標 CLI:\\n    ~/.kiro/skills, ~/.claude/skills\\n\\n  安全設計:\\n    - 預設 DRY-RUN(只印不動)。加 -Apply 才實際執行。\\n    - 移除既有 junction 用 `rmdir`(只刪連結,不碰正本內容);真實資料夾才用 Remove-Item。\\n    - 冪等:重跑只會重建連結。\\n#>\\nparam([switch]$Apply)\\n\\n$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'\\n$PersonalRoot = 'G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical'\\n$CorpRoot     = 'G:\\\\AI\\\\AI-canonical-corp'\\n$Targets = @(\\n  'C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.kiro\\\\skills',\\n  'C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\skills'\\n)\\n$DryRun = -not $Apply\\n\\nfunction Test-Reparse($path){\\n  $it = Get-Item $path -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\\n  return ($it -and ($it.Attributes -band [IO.FileAttributes]::ReparsePoint))\\n}\\nfunction Remove-Target($path){\\n  if(-not (Test-Path $path)){ return }\\n  if(Test-Reparse $path){ cmd /c rmdir \\\"$path\\\" | Out-Null }   # junction:只刪連結\\n  else { Remove-Item $path -Recurse -Force }                   # 真實資料夾\\n}\\n\\n# 掃兩個正本,建 skill 名 -> 正本路徑 對照\\n$sources = @{}\\nforeach($root in @(\\\"$PersonalRoot\\\\skills\\\",\\\"$CorpRoot\\\\skills\\\")){\\n  if(-not (Test-Path $root)){ continue }\\n  Get-ChildItem $root -Directory | ForEach-Object {           # domain\\n    Get-ChildItem $_.FullName -Directory | ForEach-Object {   # skill\\n      if($sources.ContainsKey($_.Name)){ Write-Host (\\\"[warn] 重複 skill 名: \\\" + $_.Name) -ForegroundColor Yellow }\\n      $sources[$_.Name] = $_.FullName\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nWrite-Host (\\\"[mode]    \\\" + $(if($DryRun){'DRY-RUN(只印不動;加 -Apply 才執行)'}else{'APPLY'}))\\nWrite-Host (\\\"[sources] 找到 \\\" + $sources.Count + \\\" 個 skill 正本\\\")\\n\\nforeach($t in $Targets){\\n  if(-not (Test-Path $t)){\\n    if($DryRun){ Write-Host \\\"  [would-mkdir] $t\\\" } else { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $t | Out-Null }\\n  }\\n  Write-Host \\\"`n=== target: $t ===\\\"\\n  foreach($name in ($sources.Keys | Sort-Object)){\\n    $src = $sources[$name]\\n    $dst = Join-Path $t $name\\n    $exists = Test-Path $dst\\n    $isLink = $exists -and (Test-Reparse $dst)\\n    if($DryRun){\\n      if($isLink){ Write-Host \\\"  [relink]  $name\\\" }\\n      elseif($exists){ Write-Host \\\"  [replace] $name  (現為真實資料夾 -> 改 junction)\\\" }\\n      else { Write-Host \\\"  [link]    $name\\\" }\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    Remove-Target $dst\\n    cmd /c mklink /J \\\"$dst\\\" \\\"$src\\\" | Out-Null\\n    Write-Host \\\"  [ok] $name -> $src\\\"\\n  }\\n}\\n# ─── steering(copy 投影 —— 正本為來源,edit 請改正本再 sync)───\\n#   Kiro       :開機自動載入 ~/.kiro/steering/*.md\\n#   Claude Code:無 steering 概念 → copy 到 ~/.claude/steering/,再由 CLAUDE.md @import\\n$steerSrc  = Join-Path $PersonalRoot \\\"steering\\\"\\n$steerDsts = @(\\n  \\\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.kiro\\\\steering\\\",\\n  \\\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\steering\\\"\\n)\\nif(Test-Path $steerSrc){\\n  foreach($steerDst in $steerDsts){\\n    Write-Host \\\"`n=== steering → $steerDst (copy) ===\\\"\\n    if(-not (Test-Path $steerDst)){ if(-not $DryRun){ New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $steerDst | Out-Null } }\\n    foreach($f in (Get-ChildItem $steerSrc -File -Filter *.md)){\\n      if($DryRun){ Write-Host \\\"  [would-copy] $($f.Name)\\\" }\\n      else { Copy-Item $f.FullName (Join-Path $steerDst $f.Name) -Force; Write-Host \\\"  [copied] $($f.Name)\\\" }\\n    }\\n  }\\n  # Claude Code 沒有 steering 機制 → 冪等確保全域 CLAUDE.md @import skill-workflow\\n  $claudeMd   = \\\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\CLAUDE.md\\\"\\n  $importLine = \\\"@steering/skill-workflow.md\\\"\\n  if(Test-Path $claudeMd){\\n    Write-Host \\\"`n=== CLAUDE.md @import 守衛 ===\\\"\\n    $content = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($claudeMd, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8)\\n    if($content -match [regex]::Escape($importLine)){\\n      Write-Host \\\"  [ok] CLAUDE.md 已含 $importLine\\\"\\n    } elseif($DryRun){\\n      Write-Host \\\"  [would-append] $importLine -> CLAUDE.md\\\"\\n    } else {\\n      $block = \\\"`r`n# ===================================================`r`n# Personal AI OS — Canonical Skill Workflow(投影自 steering;勿編輯投影,改正本)`r`n# ===================================================`r`n$importLine`r`n\\\"\\n      [System.IO.File]::AppendAllText($claudeMd, $block, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false))\\n      Write-Host \\\"  [appended] $importLine -> CLAUDE.md\\\"\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nif($DryRun){ Write-Host \\\"`n完成(DRY-RUN:沒有任何變更。確認後加 -Apply 執行)。\\\" }\\nelse { Write-Host \\\"`n完成(已套用 junction + steering copy)。\\\" }\\n\",\"numLines\":110,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":110}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to observe a primary Claude session. Let me parse what happened:

The user request was: "幫我查 sync.ps1 確認 steering 改 junction 會不會有衝突"

This is in Chinese (Traditional). Let me translate:
"Help me check sync.ps1 to confirm whether changing steering to junction will cause conflicts"

===========================

Claude's Full Response to User:
確認了關鍵事實:

- `~/.kiro/steering/skill-workflow.md`、`~/.claude/steering/skill-workflow.md`、`G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\skill-workflow.md` — 三份**長度都是 1671、都沒有 LinkType**。
- 也就是說它們是**三份獨立複製檔**,不是 junction/symlink。

這點很關鍵,因為你的 skills 是 **junction**(改正本即時生效),但 steering 是 **copy**(要手動 `sync.ps1` 才會更新)。兩者機制不同 — 這正是「直接寫在 `~/.kiro/steering/`」的缺點來源。

## 直接寫在 `~/.kiro/steering/skill-workflow.md` 的缺點

**1. 會被下次 sync 覆蓋(最致命)**
steering 是 copy。你跑 `G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1 -Apply` 時,它用正本內容覆寫 `~/.kiro/steering/`。你直接在這裡的編輯會**無聲消失**。這其實就是你自己 doc 裡白紙黑字的禁止事項:

> ❌ 直接在 `~/.kiro/skills/` 或 `~/.claude/skills/` 裡新增或編輯（會被下次 sync 覆蓋）

同樣邏輯完全適用 steering copy。

**2. 三份會發散,失去 single source of truth**
canonical / .kiro / .claude 是三份獨立檔。改任一份,另外兩份不同步。你的整套設計前提是「正本是唯一 source of truth,投影可重建」— 改投影就破壞了這個前提。

**3. 不進版控**
正本在 `AI-canonical` git repo(你會 commit + push,有歷史)。projection 的編輯不被追蹤、不會 push,換機或重跑 sync 就丟。

**4. 與 skills 心智模型不一致,容易誤判**
skills 改正本即時反映(junction),steering 改正本要手動 sync(copy)。你可能誤以為「改了就生效」,但 steering 其實沒 sync 就還是舊的。

## 正確作法
改 `G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\skill-workflow.md` → `sync.ps1 -Apply` → `cd G:\AI\AI-canonical && git commit && git push`。

## 但有個更上游的問題值得一起想

就算你完全照正確流程做(改正本 + sync + push),**Telegram bridge 還是讀不到 steering**(上一輪確認過,bridge 只備份不讀取)。所以對「透過 Telegram 進來的 agent」而言,steering 改了等於沒改。

順帶一提:既然 steering 跟 skills 都是「正本→投影」模型,**為什麼 skills 用 junction 而 steering 用 copy?** 如果 steering 也改成 junction,就能消掉上面缺點 1~4(改正本即時生效、無覆蓋風險、無發散)。這可能是當初設計的一個不一致點,值得你決定要不要統一。

要我幫你查 `sync.ps1` 確認 steering 改 junction 會不會有衝突(例如 `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` 的 `@steering/...` import 路徑),還是先處理 bridge 讀 steering 那個斷點?

content.thinking:
The user requested to check sync.ps1 to confirm whether changing steering from copy to junction would cause conflicts.

Looking at the observed tool execution, Claude read the sync.ps1 file to examine how steering is currently handled and whether converting it to junction would cause issues.

From Claude's response, I can see:
1. Claude confirmed that steering files in three locations (~/.kiro/steering/, ~/.claude/steering/, G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering/) are all independent copies (length 1671, no LinkType)
2. This differs from skills which use junction (changes to canonical reflect immediately)
3. Claude identified key problems with the current copy-based approach for steering
4. Claude explained why editing the projection directly is problematic
5. Claude suggested that steering could potentially be changed to junction like skills
6. Claude offered to help check sync.ps1 for potential conflicts if steering were changed to junction

The actual work done was reading and analyzing sync.ps1 to understand the current implementation and evaluate whether steering could be converted from copy to junction without conflicts.

This is a discovery type observation - learning about how the current system works and identifying a design inconsistency between skills (junction) and steering (copy).

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Steering projection uses copy instead of junction, creating sync risks</title>
  <subtitle>steering files are copied to ~/.kiro and ~/.claude, not junctioned like skills, causing potential overwrite issues</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sync.ps1 creates Windows junctions for skills from AI-canonical to ~/.kiro/skills and ~/.claude/skills</fact>
    <fact>sync.ps1 copies steering .md files from AI-canonical/steering/ to ~/.kiro/steering/ and ~/.claude/steering/</fact>
    <fact>~/.kiro/steering/skill-workflow.md, ~/.claude/steering/skill-workflow.md, and G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\skill-workflow.md are three independent 1671-byte files with no LinkType</fact>
    <fact>Direct edits to ~/.kiro/steering/ or ~/.claude/steering/ will be overwritten by next sync.ps1 -Apply run</fact>
    <fact>sync.ps1 appends @steering/skill-workflow.md import to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md if not present</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation revealed architectural inconsistency: skills use Windows junctions (changes to canonical source reflect immediately in projections), while steering uses file copies (requires manual sync.ps1 -Apply to propagate changes). This creates four key problems: (1) edits directly in ~/.kiro/steering/ or ~/.claude/steering/ get silently overwritten on next sync, violating the documented rule against editing projections; (2) three independent copies can diverge, breaking single-source-of-truth principle; (3) projection edits aren't version-controlled in the AI-canonical git repo; (4) mental model inconsistency between skills (edit-canonical-instant-effect) and steering (edit-canonical-then-sync). The sync.ps1 script handles steering in a dedicated section that copies *.md files and ensures CLAUDE.md contains the @steering/skill-workflow.md import. Converting steering to junction like skills would eliminate these risks and unify the projection model.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>G:\AI\AI-canonical\tools\sync.ps1</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<summary>
  <request>Check sync.ps1 to confirm whether converting steering from copy to junction would cause conflicts</request>
  <investigated>Read and analyzed sync.ps1 to understand current steering implementation; confirmed steering files in three locations are independent copies (1671 bytes each, no LinkType); examined the differences between how skills (junction) and steering (copy) are projected</investigated>
  <learned>sync.ps1 treats skills and steering differently: skills use Windows junctions (mklink /J) for instant reflection of canonical changes, while steering uses file copies (Copy-Item) requiring manual sync. Direct edits to steering projections (~/.kiro/steering/ or ~/.claude/steering/) will be silently overwritten on next sync run. The script also ensures ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md contains @steering/skill-workflow.md import since Claude Code lacks native steering mechanism</learned>
  <completed>Identified architectural inconsistency between skills (junction-based) and steering (copy-based) projection mechanisms; documented four key problems with copy-based approach: silent overwrites, divergence risk, no version control for projection edits, and mental model confusion</completed>
  <next_steps>Evaluate whether converting steering to junction would cause conflicts with the @steering/skill-workflow.md import path in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md; address upstream issue that Telegram bridge doesn't read steering files even after sync</next_steps>
  <notes>The investigation revealed a fundamental design inconsistency: skills achieve "edit canonical, instant effect" through junctions, while steering requires "edit canonical, then sync" due to copies. This creates maintenance burden and overwrite risks that unifying to junction-based projection could eliminate. However, need to verify junction compatibility with existing @steering/ import paths before making the change</notes>
</summary>

content.thinking:
The user is observing a session where they're checking if converting steering from copy to junction would cause conflicts. Let me analyze what was discovered:

1. Canonical source (G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\) contains:
   - .gitkeep
   - closed-loop-system.md
   - skill-workflow.md
   - task-acknowledgement.md

2. ~/.kiro/steering/ contains:
   - closed-loop-system.md
   - skill-workflow.md
   - task-acknowledgement.md
   - ui-ux-pro-max (directory!) - THIS IS KEY

3. ~/.claude/steering/ contains:
   - closed-loop-system.md
   - skill-workflow.md
   - task-acknowledgement.md

The critical discovery here is that ~/.kiro/steering/ has an EXTRA directory "ui-ux-pro-max" that doesn't exist in the canonical source. This means Kiro (or the user) has been writing additional steering content directly into the projection directory, which wouldn't exist in the canonical source.

This is a BLOCKER for converting to junction because:
1. When sync.ps1 runs with junction mode, it would Remove-Item the ~/.kiro/steering directory
2. This would DELETE the ui-ux-pro-max directory and its contents
3. That content isn't backed up in the canonical source, so it would be LOST

This is exactly the kind of conflict the user was looking for - evidence that the projection directories have been used as writable targets, not just read-only projections.

  <subtitle>~/.kiro/steering/ contains ui-ux-pro-max directory not present in canonical source, would be deleted by junction conversion</subtitle>

    <fact>ui-ux-pro-max directory exists only in ~/.kiro/steering/, indicating Kiro writes steering content directly to projection</fact>
    <fact>Converting to junction would Remove-Item ~/.kiro/steering/ and delete ui-ux-pro-max directory permanently</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Directory comparison revealed a critical blocker for junction conversion: Kiro has written custom steering content (ui-ux-pro-max directory) directly into the ~/.kiro/steering/ projection directory. This content exists nowhere else - not in canonical source, not in ~/.claude/steering/. If sync.ps1 were modified to use junctions for steering, it would execute Remove-Target on ~/.kiro/steering/ before creating the junction, permanently deleting ui-ux-pro-max and any content inside it. This proves the projection directories are being used as writable targets, not read-only mirrors. The asymmetry is revealing: ~/.claude/steering/ contains only canonical files (Claude Code doesn't generate steering), while ~/.kiro/steering/ has extra content (Kiro actively generates or accepts user-created steering files). Converting to junction requires first migrating ui-ux-pro-max back to canonical source or accepting its deletion.</narrative>

aiTitle: 檢查 sync.ps1 steering 改 junction 衝突

content.thinking:
This observation shows:
1. ui-ux-pro-max is a real directory (not a junction) - LinkType is empty
2. It contains data/, scripts/ subdirectories and a SKILL.md file
3. The canonical steering directory is git-tracked (git ls-files shows the 4 files)
4. ui-ux-pro-max is NOT in the canonical git repo

This is critical because:
- If steering were converted to junction, writes to ~/.kiro/steering/ui-ux-pro-max would actually write to G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\ui-ux-pro-max
- Since G:\AI\AI-canonical is a git repo, those writes would create untracked files in the repo
- This could pollute the canonical source with runtime-generated content

So the conflict isn't just "data loss on conversion" - it's also "junction would cause Kiro's runtime writes to pollute the git-tracked canonical source".

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>ui-ux-pro-max is real directory with skill structure, would pollute canonical git repo if junctioned</title>
  <subtitle>ui-ux-pro-max contains data/ scripts/ subdirs and SKILL.md, converting steering to junction would cause Kiro runtime writes to create untracked files in AI-canonical repo</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ui-ux-pro-max has empty LinkType and Directory attributes, confirming it is a real directory not a junction</fact>
    <fact>ui-ux-pro-max contains structured content: data/ directory, scripts/ directory, and 10547-byte SKILL.md file</fact>
    <fact>G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\ is git-tracked with 4 files: .gitkeep, closed-loop-system.md, skill-workflow.md, task-acknowledgement.md</fact>
    <fact>ui-ux-pro-max does not exist in git ls-files output for canonical steering directory</fact>
    <fact>Converting steering to junction would cause Kiro writes to ~/.kiro/steering/ui-ux-pro-max to actually write to G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\ui-ux-pro-max</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Inspection revealed ui-ux-pro-max is a real directory with skill-like structure (data, scripts subdirectories, SKILL.md file), not a junction. This creates a second-order problem for junction conversion: not only would initial conversion delete this content, but if Kiro needs to write to ~/.kiro/steering/ at runtime (which it clearly does), junction would redirect those writes to the git-tracked canonical source at G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\. This would pollute the canonical repo with untracked runtime-generated content, violating the design principle that canonical is curated source of truth. The current copy-based approach serves as write isolation - Kiro can generate steering content in projection without affecting canonical. Junction would break this isolation and require either: (1) Kiro stops generating steering files, (2) .gitignore rules to exclude runtime content from canonical, or (3) Kiro learns to generate directly in canonical and commit/push automatically.</narrative>

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Check sync.ps1 to confirm whether converting steering from copy to junction would cause conflicts</request>
  <investigated>Analyzed sync.ps1 mechanism differences; compared steering directory contents across canonical source and projections; inspected ui-ux-pro-max directory structure and git tracking status of canonical steering</investigated>
  <learned>Junction conversion has two critical conflicts: (1) immediate data loss - ui-ux-pro-max real directory with data/, scripts/, and SKILL.md would be deleted by Remove-Target during conversion; (2) runtime pollution - Kiro generates steering content at runtime and writes to ~/.kiro/steering/, junction would redirect those writes to git-tracked G:\AI\AI-canonical\steering\ creating untracked files in the canonical repo. Copy-based approach provides write isolation that junction cannot</learned>
  <completed>Confirmed steering cannot safely convert to junction without architectural changes; identified that copy mechanism serves as write isolation layer allowing Kiro to generate runtime steering content without polluting canonical source; documented that skills use junction (read-only consumption) while steering uses copy (read-write runtime generation)</completed>
  <next_steps>Architectural decision required: either keep copy-based steering to preserve write isolation, or redesign Kiro's steering generation workflow to write directly to canonical source with automatic git commit/push, or implement .gitignore rules to exclude runtime-generated steering content from version control</next_steps>
  <notes>The copy vs junction inconsistency between skills and steering is not accidental - it reflects different usage patterns. Skills are read-only consumption (CLI reads from canonical), while steering is read-write generation (Kiro generates new steering files at runtime). Converting to junction would require fundamental changes to how Kiro handles steering creation, not just a mechanical sync.ps1 modification</notes>
</summary>
